title: sesh configuration
description: Configuration file for sesh, a unified session browser for coding agents. Place at ~/.config/sesh/config.json

env.description: Environment variables applied to all commands. Per-command env overrides these.

index.description: LLM command for title generation (sesh index). Also used as fallback for other subcommands.

ask.description: LLM commands for natural language queries (sesh ask). Uses a two-pass approach: filter then generate.

command.description: Command for prose answer generation (pass 2). Fallback: ask -> recap -> index.

env.description: Environment variables for the ask command. Overrides top-level env.

filter_command.description: Command for session filtering (pass 1). Fallback: ask.filter_command -> index -> ask -> recap. Use a fast model here.

filter_env.description: Environment variables for the filter command. Overrides top-level env.

recap.description: LLM command for time-range recaps (sesh recap). Fallback: recap -> ask -> index.

providers.description: Session providers. Built-in names ("opencode", "claude") override defaults. Any other name creates an external provider.

command.description: Executable and arguments. Receives input on stdin, writes output to stdout.

prompt.description: Custom prompt to override the default.

env.description: Environment variables for this command. Overrides top-level env.

oneOf.description: Shell expression to resume a session. Use {{ID}} for session ID, {{DIR}} for directory.
Command and arguments to resume a session. Each element is individually shell-quoted.

resume_command.description: Command to resume a session. Accepts a string (raw shell expression) or an array (individually quoted arguments). Use {{ID}} for session ID, {{DIR}} for directory.

enabled.description: Whether this provider is active. Set to false to disable a built-in provider.

list_command.description: Command to list sessions (external providers only). Must output a JSON array to stdout.

env.description: Environment variables for list_command. Overrides top-level env.
